Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Hindi (code=hi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=33. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Madhya Pradesh, district=Indore.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Hindi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 33
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Transport Equipment Operators and Drivers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Madhya Pradesh
- district: Indore
- first_language: Hindi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 33-year-old female transport operator from rural Indore, Madhya Pradesh, who is currently married and has no formal education. She speaks Hindi as her first language and follows a frugal lifestyle. Her occupation involves driving light trucks and small buses, and she is known for maintaining a disciplined routine.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
डिस्चार्ज समरी — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Indore]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Suman Bai]] जन्म तिथि [[DOB|1991-05-20]] [[AGE|33]] [[GENDER|Female]]
प्रवेश हुआ [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815]] अपने पति द्वारा [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh]]
वार्ड [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] बेड [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
उपचार करने वाले [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Nair]]

रोगी 33 वर्षीय महिला है, G2P1, मध्य प्रदेश के [[DISTRICT|Indore]] जिले के एक ग्रामीण क्षेत्र से। वह पेशे से परिवहन उपकरण ऑपरेटर है और पिछले 12 घंटों से नियमित गर्भाशय संकुचन और निचले पेट दर्द की शिकायतों के साथ भर्ती हुई। उनकी पिछली मासिक धर्म अवधि 280 दिन पहले थी, और अनुमानित रूप से वह गर्भावस्था के 38वें सप्ताह में है। यह उनकी दूसरी गर्भावस्था है, जिसमें पहले एक सामान्य योनि प्रसव हुआ था। उन्हें कोई ज्ञात चिकित्सा या शल्य चिकित्सा संबंधी सह-रुग्णता नहीं है।

जांच पर, रोगी सचेत और उन्मुख थी। पेट की जांच में सिर के बल स्थित एक जीवित भ्रूण के साथ गर्भवती गर्भाशय पाया गया। ग्रीवा की जांच में 5 सेमी फैलाव, 80% विलोपन और स्टेशन -1 पाया गया। भ्रूण की हृदय गति 140 धड़कन प्रति मिनट थी। गर्भावस्था के लिए सामान्य सीमा के भीतर पूर्ण रक्त गणना, रक्त समूह और अल्ट्रासाउंड स्कैन सहित जांच की गई। उनका रक्त समूह O पॉजिटिव पाया गया।

रोगी का रूढ़िवादी तरीके से नियमित निगरानी के साथ प्रबंधन किया गया। प्रसव स्वतः ही आगे बढ़ा। उन्होंने 15 अगस्त 2024 को सुबह 08:30 बजे 3.2 किलोग्राम वजन के एक स्वस्थ पुरुष शिशु को स्वतः योनि प्रसव के माध्यम से जन्म दिया। प्रसव के तीसरे चरण का प्रबंधन ऑक्सीटोसिन इन्फ्यूजन के साथ सक्रिय रूप से किया गया। प्लेसेंटा 10 मिनट के भीतर स्वतः ही निकल गया, और गर्भाशय अच्छी तरह से संकुचित था। प्रसवोत्तर रक्तस्राव न्यूनतम था।

माता और शिशु दोनों स्थिर और ज्वरहीन हैं। रोगी को स्वच्छता, पोषण और संक्रमण के लक्षणों के बारे में सलाह दी गई है जिन पर ध्यान देना है। गर्भनिरोधक परामर्श प्रदान किया गया। 6 सप्ताह के प्रसवोत्तर के लिए अनुवर्ती अपॉइंटमेंट निर्धारित किया गया है।

[[CASTE|SC]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|suman.bai@abdm]]
generated_text_en:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Indore]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Suman Bai]] DOB [[DOB|1991-05-20]] [[AGE|33]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Admitted via [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815]] by her husband [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh]]
Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Attending [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Nair]]

Patient is a 33-year-old female, G2P1, from a rural area in [[DISTRICT|Indore]], Madhya Pradesh. She is a transport equipment operator by occupation and was admitted with complaints of regular uterine contractions and lower abdominal pain for the past 12 hours. Her last menstrual period was 280 days ago, and she is estimated to be at 38 weeks of gestation. This is her second pregnancy, with one previous normal vaginal delivery. She has no known medical or surgical comorbidities.

On examination, the patient was conscious and oriented. Abdominal examination revealed a gravid uterus with a single live fetus in cephalic presentation. Cervical examination showed 5 cm dilation, 80% effacement, and station -1. Fetal heart rate was 140 beats per minute. Investigations including a complete blood count, blood group, and an ultrasound scan were all within normal limits for pregnancy. Her blood group was found to be O positive.

The patient was managed conservatively with routine monitoring. Labor progressed spontaneously. She delivered a healthy male infant weighing 3.2 kg via spontaneous vaginal delivery at 08:30 hours on 15 August 2024. The third stage of labor was managed actively with oxytocin infusion. The placenta was delivered spontaneously within 10 minutes, and the uterus was well-contracted. Postpartum hemorrhage was minimal.

Both mother and infant are stable and afebrile. The patient is advised on postpartum care, including hygiene, nutrition, and signs of infection to watch for. Contraceptive counseling was provided. Follow-up appointment is scheduled for 6 weeks postpartum.

[[CASTE|SC]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|suman.bai@abdm]]

career_goals_and_ambitions: Ganapati aims to expand his carding operation from a small workshop to a medium‑scale unit, adopting more efficient modern machinery while retaining his hands‑on expertise. He wishes to receive formal training in textile technology, improve his English proficiency to negotiate better contracts, and eventually secure stable income for his family and education for his children. He also hopes to introduce structured planning in his work to reduce ad‑hoc disruptions, though he values the flexibility that has served him well.
persona_clinical_summary: A 40-year-old male from Karnataka, currently married, with a middle school education working as a carder in a rural cotton-processing unit. He is Kannada-speaking and prefers structured work but often resorts to last-minute fixes. His lifestyle includes consuming mangoes, listening to folk music, and occasional kabaddi play.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Automated SMS Notifications for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Haveri.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Kannada for Kannada.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 40
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Cardtenter, Cotton
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Haveri
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 40-year-old male from Karnataka, currently married, with a middle school education working as a carder in a rural cotton-processing unit. He is Kannada-speaking and prefers structured work but often resorts to last-minute fixes. His lifestyle includes consuming mangoes, listening to folk music, and occasional kabaddi play.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Gowda]]: ನಿಮ್ಮ TB ಫಾಲೋ-ಅಪ್ [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240615-02]] ರಂದು [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Haveri PHC]] ನಲ್ಲಿ ಡಾ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Kumar]] ಅವರೊಂದಿಗೆ ಇದೆ. ನಿಮ್ಮ [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] ಕಾರ್ಡ್ ತನ್ನಿ. ಖಚಿತಪಡಿಸಲು [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] ಕರೆ ಮಾಡಿ.
generated_text_en: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Gowda]]: Your TB follow-up is on [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240615-02]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Haveri PHC]] with Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Kumar]]. Please bring your [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] card. Call [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] to confirm.
judge_reasoning: Kannada prose with allowed entity tags, correct domain/persona fit, no script or type violations.

professional_persona: Ganapati Kshatri is a seasoned cardtenter who runs a modest cotton‑carding workshop, expertly maintains and troubleshoots carding machines using practical ingenuity, prefers structured planning yet often defaults to ad‑hoc fixes, and they are now charting a gradual expansion with modern equipment while valuing flexibility.
linguistic_persona: Ganapati Kshatri speaks fluent Kannada, can read and write technical documents in his mother tongue, manages functional English for basic trade communication—often sprinkling English terms into his Kannada explanations—and understands simple Hindi phrases but cannot read or write Hindi, and they occasionally mix up English spellings.
sports_persona: Ganapati Kshatri plays kabaddi with local friends after work, follows cricket avidly on television cheering for Royal Challengers Bangalore, participates in occasional village morning walks, though they sometimes skip exercise when a machine repair demands their attention.
arts_persona: Ganapati Kshatri enjoys listening to Kannada folk songs and bhajans, regularly attends Yakshagana performances featuring artists like Karkala B. Venkanna, admires veteran actor Dr. Rajkumar’s films, and collects vintage folk instrument recordings, though they rarely sing themselves.
travel_persona: Ganapati Kshatri prefers short weekend trips to nearby hill stations like Coorg and Chikmagalur for a change of scenery, occasionally plans pilgrimages to Tirupati, but they often make travel decisions spontaneously and budget‑consciously, relying on shared rides and staying with relatives.
culinary_persona: Ganapati Kshatri relishes spicy Karnataka dishes such as Bisi Bele Bath, Ragi Mudde, and Mysore Pak, enjoys fresh mangoes from his orchard especially Alphonso, prefers eating out at local dhabas serving dosa and idli, but they rarely cook at home, relying on their wife’s cooking skills.
persona: Ganapati Kshatri is a pragmatic cardtenter who blends hands‑on ingenuity with a wish for structured growth, yet often drifts into last‑minute fixes, loves folk music, mangoes, and kabaddi, and they hoard old machinery parts like quirky trophies.
cultural_background: Ganapati Kshatri is a 40‑year‑old Hindu from the agrarian belt of Haveri in Karnataka. He belongs to a Kannada‑speaking community where traditional values of family, caste identity and local customs are strong. His family has been involved in cotton processing for generations, and he continues the lineage as a cardtenter. Rural life shapes his daily routine, with festivals such as Ugadi, Sankranti and Navaratri celebrated with village gatherings, and local rituals honoring Lord Ganesha reflecting his name. The cultural milieu blends practical work‑ethic, respect for hierarchy and a love for folk arts like Yakshagana.
linguistic_background: Ganapati grew up speaking Kannada as his mother tongue; he is fully literate in Kannada, able to read, write and understand official documents and technical manuals related to textile work. He acquired functional English during his middle school years and on the job, allowing him to communicate with traders, read basic instructions, and handle simple correspondence, though his reading and writing in English remain modest. He has limited exposure to Hindi; he can understand basic Hindi phrases used in markets but cannot read or write Hindi fluently.
skills_and_expertise: Ganapati possesses practical expertise in cotton carding, operating and maintaining carding machines, ensuring yarn quality, performing routine mechanical checks, troubleshooting breakdowns using improvised solutions (jugaad), managing workflow and inventory in a modest workshop, basic bookkeeping for a small business, and effective communication with suppliers and customers in Kannada and functional English.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Carding machine operation', 'Basic mechanical maintenance', 'Quality inspection', 'Inventory management', 'Simple bookkeeping', 'Supplier negotiation', 'Jugaad problem solving']
hobbies_and_interests: Ganapati enjoys listening to Kannada folk music and bhajans, attending local Yakshagana performances, playing kabaddi with friends during evenings, watching cricket on television, maintaining a small mango orchard near his home, and tinkering with old machinery as a hobby, often using his ingenuity to repair or improve tools.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Kannada folk music', 'Attending Yakshagana performances', 'Playing kabaddi', 'Watching cricket on TV', 'Maintaining a mango orchard', 'Tinkering with old machinery']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ganapati aims to expand his carding operation from a small workshop to a medium‑scale unit, adopting more efficient modern machinery while retaining his hands‑on expertise. He wishes to receive formal training in textile technology, improve his English proficiency to negotiate better contracts, and eventually secure stable income for his family and education for his children. He also hopes to introduce structured planning in his work to reduce ad‑hoc disruptions, though he values the flexibility that has served him well.
persona_clinical_summary: A 40-year-old male cardtenter from rural Karnataka, currently married, with a middle school education. He is Kannada-speaking and works in a cotton carding workshop, often using practical ingenuity for machine repairs. His lifestyle includes listening to folk music, eating local dishes like Bisi Bele Bath, and occasional kabaddi play.

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]

  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

ರೋಗಿ ರಮೇಶ್ ಕುಮಾರ್, 40 ವರ್ಷದ ಪುರುಷ ಕಾರ್ಪೆಂಟರ್, ಗ್ರಾಮೀಣ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದವರು, ಕೆಲಸದ ಸ್ಥಳದಲ್ಲಿ ಅಪಘಾತದ ನಂತರ ಎಡ ಮುಂಗೈಗೆ ಆಘಾತಕಾರಿ ಗಾಯದೊಂದಿಗೆ ಬಂದರು. ರೋಗಿಯು ಚೂಪಾದ ವಸ್ತುವಿನಿಂದ ಉಂಟಾದ ಪಂಕ್ಚರ್ ಗಾಯ ಮತ್ತು ನಂತರದ ಮಾಲಿನ್ಯದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ವರದಿ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದಾರೆ. ಆರಂಭಿಕ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಯು ಮುಂಗೈಯ ಒಳಭಾಗದಲ್ಲಿ 2 ಸೆಂ.ಮೀ. ಉದ್ದದ ಗಾಯ ಮತ್ತು ಅದರ ಕೆಳಗೆ ಸ್ನಾಯುರಜ್ಜು (tendon) ಕಾಣಿಸುತ್ತಿರುವುದನ್ನು ಬಹಿರಂಗಪಡಿಸಿದೆ. ರೋಗಿಯು ಹೆಮೊಡೈನಾಮಿಕ್ ಆಗಿ ಸ್ಥಿರವಾಗಿದ್ದರು ಮತ್ತು ಅವರ ಪ್ರಮುಖ ಲಕ್ಷಣಗಳು ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಮಿತಿಯಲ್ಲಿದ್ದವು.

ಅಸೆಪ್ಟಿಕ್ ಪರಿಸ್ಥಿತಿಗಳಲ್ಲಿ, ರೋಗಿಯನ್ನು ಗಾಯದ ಅನ್ವೇಷಣೆ ಮತ್ತು ಡಿಬ್ರೈಡ್‌ಮೆಂಟ್‌ಗಾಗಿ ಆಪರೇಟಿಂಗ್ ಥಿಯೇಟರ್‌ಗೆ ಕರೆದೊಯ್ಯಲಾಯಿತು. ಈ ಪ್ರಕ್ರಿಯೆಯು ನಾರ್ಮಲ್ ಸಲೈನ್‌ನಿಂದ ಸೂಕ್ಷ್ಮವಾದ ತೊಳೆಯುವಿಕೆ, ಹೊರಗಿನ ವಸ್ತುಗಳನ್ನು ತೆಗೆದುಹಾಕುವುದು ಮತ್ತು ಹರಿದ ಫ್ಲೆಕ್ಸರ್ ಸ್ನಾಯುರಜ್ಜು ಕವಚದ ಪ್ರಾಥಮಿಕ ದುರಸ್ತಿಯನ್ನು ಒಳಗೊಂಡಿತ್ತು. ಪದರಗಳ ಮುಚ್ಚುವಿಕೆ (layered closure) ಮಾಡಲಾಯಿತು ಮತ್ತು ಗಾಯಕ್ಕೆ ಡ್ರೆಸ್ಸಿಂಗ್ ಮಾಡಲಾಯಿತು. ಪ್ರಕ್ರಿಯೆಯು ಯಾವುದೇ ತೊಡಕುಗಳಿಲ್ಲದೆ ಪೂರ್ಣಗೊಂಡಿತು ಮತ್ತು ರೋಗಿಯನ್ನು ವೀಕ್ಷಣೆಗಾಗಿ ಪೋಸ್ಟ್-ಆಪರೇಟಿವ್ ವಾರ್ಡ್‌ಗೆ ಸ್ಥಳಾಂತರಿಸಲಾಯಿತು.

ರೋಗಿಗೆ ಐದು ದಿನಗಳವರೆಗೆ ಇಂಟ್ರಾವೀನಸ್ ಆಂಟಿಬಯೋಟಿಕ್‌ಗಳು ಮತ್ತು ನಂತರ ಒಂದು ವಾರದವರೆಗೆ ಮೌಖಿಕ ಆಂಟಿಬಯೋಟಿಕ್‌ಗಳನ್ನು ಸೂಚಿಸಲಾಯಿತು. ಅವರಿಗೆ ನೋವು ನಿವಾರಕಗಳು ಮತ್ತು ಟೆಟಾನಸ್ ಪ್ರೊಫಿಲ್ಯಾಕ್ಸಿಸ್ ಅನ್ನು ಸಹ ಸೂಚಿಸಲಾಯಿತು. ರೋಗಿಯನ್ನು ಮೂರನೇ ಪೋಸ್ಟ್-ಆಪರೇಟಿವ್ ದಿನದಂದು ಗಾಯದ ಆರೈಕೆ ಮತ್ತು ಒಂದು ವಾರದ ನಂತರ ಸರ್ಜಿಕಲ್ ಔಟ್‌ಪೇಷಂಟ್ ವಿಭಾಗದಲ್ಲಿ ಫಾಲೋ-ಅಪ್‌ಗಾಗಿ ಸೂಚನೆಗಳೊಂದಿಗೆ ಬಿಡುಗಡೆ ಮಾಡಲಾಯಿತು. ಶಸ್ತ್ರಚಿಕಿತ್ಸೆ ಮತ್ತು ಆಸ್ಪತ್ರೆಯ ವಾಸ್ತವ್ಯದ ಒಟ್ಟು ಅಂದಾಜು ವೆಚ್ಚ ಸುಮಾರು 45,000 ರೂಪಾಯಿಗಳಾಗಿದ್ದು, ಇದನ್ನು ಒದಗಿಸಿದ ವಿಮಾ ಪಾಲಿಸಿಯ ಅಡಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ಕ್ಲೈಮ್ ಮಾಡಲಾಗುತ್ತಿದೆ. ರೋಗಿಯ ಕ್ರೆಡಿಟ್ ಕಾರ್ಡ್ [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]] (1111 ರಲ್ಲಿ ಕೊನೆಗೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತದೆ) ಆರಂಭಿಕ ಪಾವತಿಗಾಗಿ ಬಳಸಲಾಗಿದೆ ಮತ್ತು ಮರುಪಾವತಿಗಾಗಿ ಕ್ಲೈಮ್ ಪ್ರಕ್ರಿಯೆಗೊಳಿಸಲಾಗುತ್ತಿದೆ. ಖಾತೆಯ ಬ್ಯಾಂಕ್ ಪಿನ್ [[PIN|4829]] ಆಗಿದೆ. ಹೆಚ್ಚಿನ ವಿವರಗಳಿಗಾಗಿ ರೋಗಿಯನ್ನು [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] ನಲ್ಲಿ ಸಂಪರ್ಕಿಸಬಹುದು.

Patient Ramesh Kumar, a 40-year-old male cardtenter from rural Karnataka, presented with a traumatic injury to the left forearm following a workplace accident at the cotton carding unit. The patient reported a sharp object puncture wound with subsequent contamination. Initial examination revealed a 2 cm laceration over the volar aspect of the forearm with underlying tendon exposure. The patient was hemodynamically stable with vitals within normal limits.

Under aseptic conditions, the patient was taken to the operating theatre for exploration and debridement of the wound. The procedure involved meticulous irrigation with normal saline, removal of foreign bodies, and primary repair of the lacerated flexor tendon sheath. A layered closure was performed, and the wound was dressed. The procedure was uncomplicated, and the patient was shifted to the post-operative ward for observation.

The patient was advised a course of intravenous antibiotics for five days, followed by oral antibiotics for a week. He was also prescribed analgesics and tetanus prophylaxis. The patient was discharged on the third post-operative day with instructions for wound care and follow-up at the surgical outpatient department in one week. The total estimated cost of the surgery and hospitalization was approximately Rs. 45,000, which is being claimed under the provided insurance policy. The patient's credit card [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]] ending in 1111 was used for the initial payment, and the claim is being processed for reimbursement. The bank PIN for the account is [[PIN|4829]]. The patient can be contacted at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] for any further details.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Hospital Billing / Invoice for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Haveri.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Kannada for Kannada.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 40
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Cardtenter, Cotton
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural

He is Kannada-speaking and resides in Haveri district. His lifestyle includes consuming local cuisine, listening to folk music, and occasional participation in kabaddi.

Document format guide for Hospital Billing / Invoice (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (English pivot; wrap facility/geo as tags — never plain-only):

Receipt No. INV-2024-0815 (plain prose — NOT a TYPE). State line as plain prose (NOT [[STATE|…]]).
Line items with amounts (no entity tags on procedure names).
TYPE aliases forbidden: ADDRESS_NUMBER→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS;
RECEIPT_NUMBER→plain; STATE→plain or DISTRICT.

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]

- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ರೋಗಿಯ ಇತಿಹಾಸ ಹೀಗಿದೆ: 4 ದಿನಗಳ ನಿರಂತರ ಕೆಮ್ಮು, ಜ್ವರ ಮತ್ತು ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಉಸಿರಾಟದ ತೊಂದರೆ. ಅವರು ಹೆಚ್ಚಿನ ಧೂಳಿನ ಸಂಪರ್ಕದೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹತ್ತಿ ಜಿನ್ನಿಂಗ್ ಘಟಕದಲ್ಲಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾರೆ ಎಂದು ವರದಿ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದಾರೆ. ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಯ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ, ಅವರ ತಾಪಮಾನ 101.8°F, ನಾಡಿಮಿಡಿತ ನಿಮಿಷಕ್ಕೆ 96, ಉಸಿರಾಟದ ದರ ನಿಮಿಷಕ್ಕೆ 22 ಮತ್ತು ರಕ್ತದೊತ್ತಡ 135/85 mmHg ಇತ್ತು. ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಗಾಳಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ಆಮ್ಲಜನಕದ ಸ್ಯಾಚುರೇಶನ್ 96% ಇತ್ತು. ಎದೆಯ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಚದುರಿದ ರೋಂಕಿ ಕಂಡುಬಂದಿತು. ವೃತ್ತಿಪರ ಸಂಪರ್ಕದೊಂದಿಗೆ ತೀವ್ರ ಬ್ರಾಂಕೈಟಿಸ್‌ನ ತಾತ್ಕಾಲಿಕ ರೋಗನಿರ್ಣಯವನ್ನು ಮಾಡಲಾಯಿತು. ಕೆಲಸದ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ ಮಾಸ್ಕ್ ಬಳಸಲು, ಜ್ವರಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ಪ್ಯಾರಸಿಟಮಾಲ್ ತೆಗೆದುಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಮತ್ತು ಅಮೋಕ್ಸಿಸಿಲಿನ್ ಕೋರ್ಸ್ ಅನ್ನು ಸೂಚಿಸಲಾಯಿತು. ಅವರಿಗೆ ಉಸಿರಾಟದ ನೈರ್ಮಲ್ಯದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಸಲಹೆ ನೀಡಲಾಯಿತು.

Patient presented with a 4-day history of persistent cough, fever, and mild shortness of breath. He reports working in a cotton ginning unit with significant dust exposure. system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Automated SMS Notifications for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Konkani (code=kok, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Uttara Kannada.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Konkani.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 26
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Driver, Bus
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Uttara Kannada
- first_language: Konkani
- clinical_persona_summary: A 26-year-old male bus driver from rural Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, who is never married and has completed higher secondary education. He is fluent in Konkani and Kannada, and works as a driver for a bus service. His lifestyle includes a habit of checking vehicle fuel gauges and a preference for off-duty road trips to nearby coastal towns.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Patil]]: तुमचो मानसोपचार पाठपुरावा चिंता व्यवस्थापना खातीर आसा. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-241120-03]] 20-नोव्हेंबर क 11:00 वेळार [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Rao]] [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Karwar]] हांचे वांगडा. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]. खात्री करपा खातीर [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] फोन करात.
generated_text_en: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Patil]]: Your psychiatry follow-up for anxiety management is [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-241120-03]] on 20-Nov at 11:00 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Rao]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Karwar]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]. Call [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] to confirm.
judge_reasoning: Konkani Devanagari prose correct; all TYPEs allow-listed; male 26 psychiatry fit; short SMS length; Latin IDs inside tags expected.

professional_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal, a 26‑year‑old bus driver in Uttara Kannada, blends meticulous route‑planning and disciplined vehicle maintenance with a sociable, team‑oriented demeanor, ensuring punctual and safe journeys for his passengers.
linguistic_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal fluently speaks Konkani and Kannada using the Kannada script, and can read and write English in the Latin script, allowing them to handle village paperwork, passenger tickets, and road signs with ease.
sports_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal regularly joins village cricket matches, cheering for the Karnataka state team and the local Karwar Cricket Club, and maintains fitness through quick sprints between bus stops, though they admit to skipping formal gym sessions.
arts_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal enjoys the rhythmic beats of Konkani folk songs by singers like Laxman Goud, assists backstage in Yakshagana performances, and occasionally watches Bollywood classics starring Madhuri Dixit and Amitabh Bachchan for inspiration.
travel_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal prefers off‑duty road trips to nearby towns like Mangalore and Goa, planning itineraries on the fly while keeping costs low, and dreams of exploring the backwaters of Kerala during a future holiday break.
culinary_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal loves coastal delicacies such as bangda (mackerel) curry, neer dosa with coconut chutney, and sol kadhi, often savoring the tangy taste of kokum and tamarind, and can whip up a simple fish fry with mustard seeds and curry leaves when at home.
persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal is a disciplined bus driver who balances precise route‑planning with lively Konkani bhajan humming, but has a habit of obsessively checking the bus’s fuel gauge and occasionally over‑engineering fixes with duct‑tape.
cultural_background: Basavaraj hails from a rural coastal community in Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, where Konkani culture blends with the broader Kannada milieu. His family follows traditional Hindu customs, celebrates coastal festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi and Ugadi, and participates in folk arts such as Yakshagana and Konkani bhajans. The daily rhythm revolves around agriculture, fishing, and the bus routes that connect villages to towns, reflecting a pragmatic, community‑oriented way of life.
linguistic_background: Basavaraj is fluent in Konkani, his mother tongue, and uses the Kannada script for reading and writing it. He is also fully proficient in Kannada, the state language, both in spoken and written form, which enables him to communicate with locals and manage official paperwork. His education up to Higher Secondary gave him functional proficiency in English, allowing him to understand road signs, fill out transport logs, and interact with passengers from diverse backgrounds. Overall, he reads and writes comfortably in Konkani (Kannada script), Kannada (Kannada script), and English (Latin script).
skills_and_expertise: Basavaraj possesses solid bus‑driving expertise, including safe handling of a heavy passenger vehicle on varied rural and highway roads. He is skilled in route planning, punctual schedule adherence, and passenger management, ensuring a calm and orderly journey. His practical knowledge extends to routine vehicle maintenance, basic mechanical troubleshooting, and emergency response. He demonstrates strong interpersonal communication in Konkani, Kannada and English, and employs disciplined record‑keeping for logs, fares, and safety checks. His organized mindset aids in optimizing fuel efficiency and managing daily operational challenges.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Bus driving", "Vehicle maintenance", "Route planning", "Customer service", "Time management", "Interpersonal communication", "Multilingual communication (Konkani, Kannada, English)", "Basic mechanical troubleshooting"]
hobbies_and_interests: Basavaraj enjoys participating in village cricket matches and listening to Konkani folk songs that remind him of coastal celebrations. He takes part in Yakshagana performances during festivals, both as a backstage helper and occasional performer. In his free time, he likes tinkering with his bus engine using simple mutta (jugaad) solutions, reading devotional literature, and exploring nearby towns on off‑duty routes. Socialising with fellow drivers over tea, cooking traditional coastal dishes, and attending local fairs also feature prominently in his leisure.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Playing cricket (village team)", "Listening to Konkani folk music", "Participating in Yakshagana performances", "Vehicle tinkering (jugaad)", "Reading devotional literature", "Exploring nearby towns on off‑duty routes", "Socialising with fellow drivers over tea", "Cooking coastal dishes"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Basavaraj aims to upgrade his professional credentials by completing a certified Advanced Driver Training program and obtaining a Commercial Passenger Vehicle licence. He aspires to become a senior bus driver, responsible for training junior conductors and overseeing route safety. In the longer term, he wishes to manage a small fleet of intra‑district buses or open his own transport service, providing reliable connectivity for rural villages. His goals reflect his desire for structured advancement, higher earnings to support his family, and contributing to safer, more punctual public transport in his region.
persona_clinical_summary: A 26-year-old male bus driver from rural Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, who is never married, holds a higher secondary education and is fluent in both Konkani and Kannada. He maintains a disciplined lifestyle with a habit of over-engineering vehicle repairs and frequently checks the fuel gauge. His cultural background is rooted in a rural coastal community, and he is actively involved in local Konkani bhajan singing.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Hospital Billing / Invoice for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Konkani (code=kok, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Uttara Kannada.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Konkani.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 26
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Driver, Bus
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Uttara Kannada
- first_language: Konkani
- clinical_persona_summary: A 26-year-old male bus driver from rural Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, who is never married, holds a higher secondary education and is fluent in both Konkani and Kannada. He maintains a disciplined lifestyle with a habit of over-engineering vehicle repairs and frequently checks the fuel gauge. His cultural background is rooted in a rural coastal community, and he is actively involved in local Konkani bhajan singing.

Document format guide for Hospital Billing / Invoice (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (English pivot; wrap facility/geo as tags — never plain-only):
Invoice — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Barddhaman District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Purba Bardhaman]]
PIN [[PIN_CODE|713101]] Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] MRN [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-0815]]
Address [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Station Road, Barddhaman]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|anjali.mondal@example.com]] PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
Landline [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0342-1234567]] Vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|WB40AB1234]] (once)
Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|291512345678]] Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-WB-7781]] GSTIN [[TAX_ID|19ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
Receipt No. INV-2024-0815 (plain prose — NOT a TYPE). State line as plain prose (NOT [[STATE|…]]).
Line items with amounts (no entity tags on procedure names).
TYPE aliases forbidden: ADDRESS_NUMBER→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS;
RECEIPT_NUMBER→plain; STATE→plain or DISTRICT.

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PIN_CODE — ZIP / Postal Code (PIN Code) e.g. [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- TAX_ID — Tax Identification Number (TIN/GSTIN) e.g. [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
- TELEPHONE_LANDLINE — Telephone Number (Landline) e.g. [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

His practical knowledge extends to routine vehicle maintenance, basic mechanical troubleshooting, and emergency response. system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
